summary: McKenzie demonstrates the business value of blogging for micro-ISVs by sharing concrete results from a single blog post. His 'Free Bingo Cards' post generated nearly 1,000 hits in one month, converting to about 100 trials and 5 sales—roughly 20% of his monthly revenue. He emphasizes that the post is 'evergreen' content that continues generating traffic and sales indefinitely through search engines and sharing.
key_points: A single well-targeted blog post generated 20% of monthly sales two months after publication
Evergreen content compounds in value over time through search engine hits, links, and word-of-mouth sharing
The conversion funnel from blog post was roughly 1,000 hits → 100 trials → 5 sales (10% trial rate, 5% conversion)
Blogging creates a durable marketing asset that 'never goes bad' unlike paid advertising
money_quotes: That post is an evergreen — it never goes bad.
It continues to pick up search engine hits, links, emails to friends, yadda yadda, and keeps making me money.
This resulted in ~100 trials of my program and 5 sales, or roughly 20% of my sales for the month.
key_insight: A single piece of evergreen blog content can become a compounding marketing asset that generates a significant share of ongoing revenue with zero marginal cost.
